items.evidence_text:
Client: Hi Alex. I’m self-employed, income’s been solid, and I want a tighter plan—especially around taxes and making sure retirement at 63 is actually on track.
Advisor: Got it. And you said you like it efficient, so I’ll keep this moving. Let’s start with income—what are you running for gross household income per year right now?
Client: Gross is 177750 a year.
Advisor: Okay. Is that mostly business income, or do you think of it as salary plus bonus—how does it hit your accounts?
Client: It’s basically a monthly bonus-style draw. Same idea—comes in monthly, but annualized it’s 177750.

Client: It’s basically a monthly bonus-style draw. Same idea—comes in monthly, but annualized it’s 177750.
Advisor: Perfect. Next, spending—what do your total monthly expenses look like, all-in?
Client: Around six-ish. The number I use is 6150 per month.
Advisor: 6,150 a month—thanks. Does that include housing costs and insurance premiums, or are those separate buckets for you?
Client: That includes the usual bills and the house stuff. The life insurance premium is separate in my head, but it’s still part of the monthly outflow.

Client: Hi Alex. I’m self-employed, income’s been solid, and I want a tighter plan—especially around taxes and making sure retirement at 63 is actually on track.
Advisor: Got it. And you said you like it efficient, so I’ll keep this moving. Let’s start with income—what are you running for gross household income per year right now?
Client: Gross is 177750 a year.
Advisor: Okay. Is that mostly business income, or do you think of it as salary plus bonus—how does it hit your accounts?
Client: It’s basically a monthly bonus-style draw. Same idea—comes in monthly, but annualized it’s 177750.

Client: Yeah, no liabilities right now.
Advisor: Great. Let’s shift to assets. Roughly how much do you have in investable assets—so excluding the home value for a second?
Client: Investable assets total is 965900.
Advisor: Thanks—hang on, I’m just noting that. And then you do also own your primary residence—what’s the value you use for that?
Client: 500600.

Client: Investable assets total is 965900.
Advisor: Thanks—hang on, I’m just noting that. And then you do also own your primary residence—what’s the value you use for that?
Client: 500600.
Advisor: Okay. Now I want to understand what makes up that 965,900. Do you have a taxable account?
Client: Yes—taxable account is 522650. It’s held through an insurance company platform.

Advisor: Okay. And what state do you consider your primary residence for tax and legal purposes?
Client: Other.
Advisor: Perfect. Switching gears a bit—how are you engaging an advisor? Like, are you looking for an RIA relationship, brokerage, something else?
Client: RIA (registered investment advisor).
Advisor: Great—thank you. Let me just note that down. Anything about that model you care most about—fiduciary standard, fees, or just clean implementation?

Client: Hi Alex. I’m self-employed, income’s been solid, and I want a tighter plan—especially around taxes and making sure retirement at 63 is actually on track.

Client: It’s basically a monthly bonus-style draw. Same idea—comes in monthly, but annualized it’s 177750.

Advisor: Okay. And specifically on the house: do you have any mortgage balance left, or is that paid off?
Client: Mortgage balance outstanding is 0.
Advisor: Got it. Any other debt at all—credit cards you carry month to month, car note, student loans—anything non-mortgage?
Client: Non-mortgage debt outstanding is 0.
Advisor: Perfect. Just so I don’t mix terms—when you said earlier “monthly debt payments total is 0,” that lines up with those balances being zero too.

Client: Correct.
Advisor: Alright, Daniel—thanks. I want to grab a couple household basics so I don’t make assumptions. First, what’s your marital status right now?
Client: single.
Advisor: Got it. And any dependents—kids or anyone financially dependent on you?
Client: No—0.

Client: 1.
Advisor: Perfect. On taxes—what bracket do you generally plan around these days? I know it can move year to year.
Client: 24%.
Advisor: Okay. And risk-wise, when we’re talking about the portfolio and the plan—how would you describe your risk tolerance in plain English?
Client: moderate.

Advisor: Hi Daniel—good to meet you. I’m Alex. Before we get into the weeds, what prompted you to reach out now?
